summary: Matched and linked the item compilation unit for itfushigibana. The PR changed one ItemStateTable value, moved two identical static helper definitions earlier in itfushigibana.c, corrected two .sdata2 symbols from global it_804DD580/it_804DD584 names to local @154/@155 labels, and promoted the object from NonMatching to Matching. The decomp.dev report confirmed +1024 linked code bytes and new 100% matches for the unit's .sdata2 and .data sections.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source; changed the third ItemStateTable entry first field from 2 to -1 and moved itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline1/inline2 earlier in the file without changing their bodies.
Symbol table update; changed .sdata2 constants at 0x804DD580 and 0x804DD584 from global it_804DD580/it_804DD584 symbols to local @154/@155 labels.
Build configuration update; changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itfushigibana.c") to Object(Matching, ...).

what_changed: In ItemStateTable it_803F82F0, the third motion entry's first value changed from 2 to -1.

Static helpers itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline1 and itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline2 were moved from after it_802D718C to near the top of itfushigibana.c before itFushigibana_Logic29_Spawned; their function bodies stayed the same in the diff.
The .sdata2 symbols it_804DD580 and it_804DD584 were reclassified as local labels @154 and @155.
configure.py now treats melee/it/items/itfushigibana.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
The bot reported linked code improving by +1024 bytes and matched/linked data improving by +56 bytes for GALE01.
smart_moves: The PR finished the match as a complete unit-level change: C source, symbol metadata, and configure.py were updated together so the object could be linked.
Reclassifying two float constants as local @NNN labels targeted .sdata2 matching and avoided leaving unnecessary global it_804DDxxx symbols.
The source change preserved behavior for the two helper bodies and used placement/order plus a table literal correction as low-noise matching fixes.
The final configure.py promotion was backed by the bot report showing the itfushigibana .sdata2 section at 100% and .data section at 100%.
decomp_lessons: For item unit data matches, small ItemStateTable literals can be decisive; verify unknown motion/state table values against the original binary instead of assuming sequential IDs.
When .sdata2 remains unmatched, check whether constants should be local @NNN labels rather than exported it_804xxxxx globals.

In MWCC-style matching work, moving identical static helper definitions can be a code/object layout tactic; this PR provides evidence of such a move but no reviewer explanation of the exact compiler effect.
Only mark an item source file as Matching in configure.py after source and symbol changes account for the relevant code and data sections.
naming_conventions: Item-specific callbacks use the itFushigibana_ prefix, for example itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim, _Phys, and _Coll.
Unrenamed address-style routines remain in the it_802Dxxxx form, for example it_802D718C and it_802D7328.
Static helper names in this file encode the caller context and inline index: itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline1 and itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline2.
Local .sdata2 constants in symbols.txt may use @NNN labels, while remaining unresolved/exported globals use it_804DDxxx-style names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed a single ItemStateTable constant from 2 to -1 to improve .data matching for it_803F82F0.
Converted .sdata2 entries at 0x804DD580 and 0x804DD584 from global symbols to local labels, which corresponded with the bot's .sdata2 new match report.
Moved static helper definitions earlier in the translation unit; the diff does not explain whether the benefit was function ordering, inlining visibility, or another MWCC codegen sensitivity.
Promoted the object to Matching only after the source/symbol corrections made the unit linkable.
review_feedback: No human PR body, reviews, or review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot report for GALE01 (77db288 - 2c93910): linked code 31.03% (+0.03%, +1024 bytes), matched data 20.99% (+0.00%, +56 bytes), linked data 28.88% (+0.00%, +56 bytes).
Bot listed 2 new matches for main/melee/it/items/itfushigibana: .sdata2 +4 bytes from 50.00% to 100.00%, and .data +3 bytes from 95.24% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that convert item objects from NonMatching to Matching with small ItemStateTable changes.
Search GALE01/symbols.txt history for .sdata2 globals converted to @NNN local labels.
Look for other item files where static _inline1/_inline2 helpers were moved to satisfy matching.
Check nearby Pokemon item decomp PRs for similar ItemStateTable first-field values of -1 versus sequential motion IDs.
